behavior_summary: U8P_INTERN::look responds to a 'U8P_INTERN::look' (Event 0) trigger by causing the referenced process/object to emit a short descriptive message.

The handler builds the literal text "Human Remains ", arranges call context flags (set info / process exclude), calls Item::bark on the target process to display that text, frees the temporary string, inspects the bark return value via an implies/pop sequence, and then suspends/returns.
Effect in gameplay: when the player (or relevant actor) examines the INTERN object, they are shown the short description "Human Remains ".
notable_control_flow: Entry on Event 0 (U8P_INTERN::look): minimal init then create temporary string 'Human Remains '.
Set context flags (inferred: 'set info' and 'process exclude') to control recipient/context before invoking Item::bark.
Call Item::bark with the target process (inferred BP+06h) and the constructed string; free the string afterwards.
Capture Item::bark return, feed it through an implies/pop sequence (exact effect unclear), then suspend and return.
open_questions: What precisely does the implies/pop sequence do with Item::bark's return value (flow control, conditional suppression, or engine bookkeeping)?
What exact semantic effect do the context flags 'set info' and 'process exclude' have for this call (which processes see the bark or are excluded)?
Is BP+06h always the player/process that triggered the U8P_INTERN::look, or can it be other processes (NPCs, scripts) in some contexts?

main_function.name: U8P_INTERN::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the object's 'U8P_INTERN::look' (examine) event by producing a short on-screen description ('Human Remains ') via Item::bark, then perform minimal cleanup and return.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_INTERN::look) — triggered when an actor (typically the player) examines or looks at the INTERN object.

function_map.name: U8P_INTERN::look
function_map.role: Event handler for the object's 'U8P_INTERN::look' action; prepares context, issues Item::bark with a literal description, and returns.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item routine used to display a short text message or description originating from an item/process.

external_calls.purpose: Request the target process/object to emit/display the short description string 'Human Remains ' to the viewer/process context.

writes.name: set info (context key, inferred 0x210 usage)
writes.likely_effect: Appearsto set an info/context bit on the calling or target process to influence the subsequent bark call or processing.

writes.name: process exclude (contextual exclusion)
writes.likely_effect: Likely excludes certain processes or the caller from further handling or receiving the bark; used to control recipients/context of the message.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_INTERN::look responds to a 'U8P_INTERN::look' (Event 0) trigger by causing the referenced process/object to emit a short descriptive message.
